name: Procurement negotiation
description: A high-stakes supply-contract negotiation. YOU are the BUYER; whispers coach you privately. Designed to fire every agent in the Negotiation suite and to show: an anchoring alert + Phase-2 concession tracker (event coordination), a BATNA reminder on an INTERVAL tick, deadline pressure that gets cooldown-gated, and a priority collision on the `phase` variable.
user_context: You are the head of procurement at a manufacturer. You are negotiating a one-year supply contract with a vendor's sales director. Your copilot whispers private coaching to you. Your BATNA is a competing supplier at $42/unit.

steps.speaker: ME
steps.text: Thanks for coming in. Before we get into anything, walk me through what your team can actually commit to on lead times.
steps.note: Opening. No bargaining/closing keywords match, so both phase agents hit their empty-when fallback: Phase Tracker writes phase=phase_tracker_says_opening (prio 1), Deal Strategist writes phase=opening (prio 9). Strategist writes LAST -> Blackboard `phase` = 'opening'. This is the priority collision; watch the value land as the strategist's, not the tracker's. No whisper.

steps.speaker: SELLER
steps.text: Happy to. We're confident on delivery, and the quality is the best you'll find at this tier.
steps.note: Seller talks value but drops NO number and uses no phase keyword. Still 'opening' (collision resolves to strategist's 'opening' again). Anchoring Alert sees no number -> silent. Quiet turn.

steps.speaker: SELLER
steps.text: For your volume we can do $58 per unit. That's our standard for a one-year commitment.
steps.note: SELLER DROPS THE FIRST NUMBER. Expect: phase -> 'bargaining' (both agents match 'per unit'; Strategist's value wins the collision); Anchoring Alert WARNS + writes an `anchor` fact + emits the `number_anchored` EVENT; and in PHASE 2 the Concession Tracker fires on has_event, whispers, writes a `concession` fact, and pushes item 1 onto the `give_gets` queue. THIS is the event-coordination turn (Phase 1 -> Phase 2, one round-trip cost).

steps.speaker: ME
steps.text: That's well above where I need to land. Tell me how you got to that number.
steps.note: Buyer pushes back, no number, no phase keyword -> falls through to 'opening' on the empty-when rule. (Subtle lesson: the phase agents are memoryless per turn -- with nothing matching, they reset to the fallback bucket. phase flips opening<->bargaining as keywords come and go.) No whisper.

steps.speaker: SELLER
steps.text: Honestly the deadline matters here: this price expires Friday, then the discount is gone.
steps.note: FIRST deadline-pressure hit, routed as a KEYWORD trigger ('deadline'/'expires'). The host keyword-matches and only routes matching agents; here the allow-list = [Deadline Pressure Detector] (Anchoring's keywords don't appear in this line). It WARNS, writes a `tactic` fact, and pressure_count -> 1 in private memory. The phase trackers are turn_based-only, so they're skipped 'not_in_allow_list' and `phase` does not change this turn.

steps.speaker: SELLER
steps.text: I mean it -- treat this as our final offer, the deadline is firm.
steps.note: SECOND deadline hit ('final offer'/'deadline') only 6s after the first, again a KEYWORD trigger. The word 'offer' also matches Anchoring Alert's keywords, so the host allow-list this turn is [Anchoring Alert, Deadline Pressure Detector] -- but Anchoring's rule needs a $-number regex, which this line lacks, so it stays silent (a free lesson: keyword ROUTING is not the same as rule FIRING). Deadline Pressure Detector IS routed and keyword-eligible, BUT its 9s cooldown has not elapsed (only 6s since it fired) -> it is GATED. Watch 'skipped: cooldown': routed and matched, yet it does not run. Cooldown is checked AFTER eligibility.

steps.speaker: SELLER
steps.text: Tell you what -- knock it to $51 per unit if you commit now. That's a real discount.
steps.note: Seller moves to a NEW number, 35s after the first anchor (past the 8s Anchoring cooldown). Back to a turn_based turn. phase -> 'bargaining' ('per unit'/'discount'). Anchoring Alert fires AGAIN -> emits `number_anchored` -> Concession Tracker fires again in Phase 2, pushing item 2 onto `give_gets`. The concession ledger grows to 2.

steps.speaker: ME
steps.text: I appreciate the move, but I need to weigh this against my other options. Give me a second.
steps.note: Buyer reflects, no number, no phase keyword -> 'opening' fallback again. No coach fires. This pause sets up the interval tick next.

INTERVAL tick during the buyer's pause (no text). ONLY the BATNA Reminder subscribes to INTERVAL, so it is the lone agent routed -- every turn_based / keyword / event agent is skipped (trigger_type_mismatch). It whispers the $42/unit walk-away. The lesson: trigger TYPE is routing, fully independent of keywords or content.

steps.speaker: ME
steps.text: Okay. I can commit if you can. Where does the price land if I sign an 18-month term?
steps.note: Buyer trades term for price -- exactly the 'give to get' the Concession Tracker coached. 'the price' matches the bargaining bucket, so phase -> 'bargaining'. No number from the buyer, so anchoring/concession stay quiet (the regex needs a seller number).

steps.speaker: SELLER
steps.text: An 18-month term changes things. I can get you to $47 per unit at that length.
steps.note: Seller concedes a THIRD number, well past the 8s anchoring cooldown. phase -> 'bargaining'. Anchoring Alert fires -> `number_anchored` -> Concession Tracker Phase 2 again, pushing item 3. The `give_gets` queue is now 3 deep: the running concession ledger the buyer can review at close.

steps.speaker: ME
steps.text: That works for me. Let's sign the deal and send the paperwork over today.
steps.note: Agreement. 'sign the deal' + 'send the paperwork' -> closing bucket. phase collision resolves to 'closing' (Strategist prio 9 beats Phase Tracker's 'phase_tracker_says_closing'). No number, so anchoring stays silent. Clean close with the full fact set + 3-item concession ledger on the Blackboard.
